top_issues.issue: Missing require('crypto') import — crypto.createHash() used as implicit global, causing ReferenceError at runtime on WebSocket upgrade path
Flawed error-catch test pattern: .catch((err) => should.not.exist(err)).finally(done) silently swallows assertion failures in 6 tests
Flawed error-catch test pattern: .catch((err) => should.not.exist(err)).finally(done) silently swallows assertion failures in 9 tests
upsert() bypasses applyBody helper and directly serializes JSON body, silently producing incorrect requests when upserting binary SObjects (Document, ContentVersion)
Module-level mutable arrays (serverStack, requestStack) shared across all test imports — cross-test state contamination risk
Prototype mixin via Object.assign(Connection.prototype, httpMethods, authMethods, apiMethods) creates 35+ method flat surface and Divergent Change risk
Seven assignment statements missing space after = operator (lines 226, 240, 241, 255, 257, 271, 272) — inconsistent style across file
_notifyAndResolve uses callback-based onRefresh API inside Promise constructor while all other library APIs are Promise-only
Entire file uses double-quoted strings while rest of codebase enforces single quotes — violates ESLint quotes rule
Duplicated re-subscription loop in both _rehandshake() and _scheduleReconnect() — should be extracted to _resubscribeAll()
